supported_claims: Fireworks AI provides highly reliable and low-latency execution of multi-step workflows with near-zero retry rates compared to baseline models.

Fireworks AI supports advanced large-context models like MiniMax-M1-80k with native support for up to 1 million tokens, enabling complex use cases such as multi-file code refactoring, legal document analysis, and multi-turn agent workflows.
Fireworks AI offers efficient compute architectures (e.g., hybrid MoE + lightning attention) that reduce FLOPs by up to 75% for long generations, improving speed and cost-effectiveness.

Fireworks AI runs model weights directly on its platform, providing end-to-end serving with zero data retention and production-grade latency, unlike inference providers that forward calls to external endpoints.

Fireworks AI collaborates with technologies like MongoDB Atlas and Whisper V3 Turbo to build enterprise-scale Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that handle unstructured data with real-time semantic search and fast audio transcription.
what_not_to_claim: Any user pain points or feature requests, as the evidence is from official marketing posts only.
Comparisons that are not explicitly supported by the provided data, such as direct claims of superiority over all competitors beyond the stated benchmarks.
Any negative feedback or churn indications, as none are present in the marketing evidence.

chosen.description: Official marketing and employee posts confirm that Fireworks AI maintains stable, low-latency execution across complex multi-step workflows, supporting large-context models up to 1 million tokens with near-zero retry rates. The platform's efficient compute architectures reduce FLOPs by up to 75%, enabling cost-effective, production-grade performance with zero data retention.
chosen.recommended_action: Leverage these technical proofs in marketing to highlight Fireworks AI's unique capabilities in handling complex, large-context workflows with superior latency and efficiency.
chosen.title: Fireworks AI delivers stable low-latency multi-step workflow execution with advanced large-context support and efficient compute

All code, docs, and tests are open source → https://t.co/6wTxAJLErY", "rewritten_text": null, "snippet": "The stack is described as low-latency, high-throughput, and cost-efficient.", "engagement": 1099.0, "metrics": {"likes": 16, "views": 1069, "shares": 0, "num_comments": 14}, "created_at": "2026-06-29T07:01:20.494792+00:00", "metadata": {"processing_status": "classified", "source_type": "crawl", "content_hash": "39eab9ebb83b1bd576953c210158a0beedf541afc210ea36cf0f98548e2ff4ec"}}, {"source_id": "bcb0ec31-257f-45d9-bd5a-4ac15c1dca88", "opinion_unit_id": "1845dbe1-ae92-4d14-a24a-83c745f3e204", "platform": "twitter", "url": "https://x.com/FireworksAI_HQ/status/2006930964224815155", "title": "Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ", "author": "FireworksAI_HQ", "sender_type_current": "official", "source_group_current": "marketing", "source_class_current": null, "source_role_predicted": "official_brand", "source_group_predicted": "owned_official", "content_type_predicted": "technical_proof", "feedback_type_predicted": ["none"], "text": "Everyone is talking about @deepseek_ai's mHC (Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections) paper; let’s break it down in simple language.

What they’re trying to do: A model has one “main highway,” the residual stream, which carries information through all layers. DeepSeek splits that highway into a few parallel lanes so different lanes can carry different kinds of information, and the model can merge that traffic as it goes.

The problem: If every layer is allowed to freely “turn the knobs” on how much each lane is amplified, those knobs can compound over multiple layers. 
The result: Some lanes get too much traffic, others remain empty, and training becomes unstable.

DeepSeek added a simple rule: each layer can redistribute information across lanes but not amplify it overall, thereby conserving the information flow. To mix our metaphors slightly, think of it as a mixer board. You can move sound between channels, but you can’t secretly crank the overall volume. 

The non-obvious win: This rule holds even when stacking many layers. Instead of small mistakes that add up and explode, you get stable mixing all the way down, while still benefiting from multiple lanes.

What you get in practice: training becomes much more predictable, and quality improves across benchmarks (final loss reduction of 0.021 vs the baseline on a 27B LLM).

Why it matters for real systems: This kind of multi-lane design can be expensive to run. DeepSeek also did engineering work on custom kernels and a smarter backward pass, so it’s only ~7 percent slower to train with four lanes, which is surprisingly practical.

Net: More expressive residual connections, without the usual instability, and the overhead is small enough to actually use.

This is really impressive research. We’re excited to see the next generation of models built on top of this architecture. In the meantime, if you’re ready to try today’s DeepSeek models (and other frontier open weight models), you can get started with one click. You can run them on the Fireworks Inference Cloud, which delivers open-source models at blazing speed, optimized for your use case, and scaled globally: https://t.co/e8syugMkdp", "rewritten_text": null, "snippet": "The engineering work keeps the training overhead small enough to be practical, with only about 7 percent slower training for four lanes.", "engagement": 1553.0, "metrics": {"likes": 18, "views": 1533, "shares": 0, "num_comments": 2}, "created_at": "2026-06-29T07:01:16.029637+00:00", "metadata": {"processing_status": "classified", "source_type": "crawl", "content_hash": "31b73bd8e7ac038728299400f27eda45bcf5fde7bf13b0cca2d7c2c0920c24f5"}}, {"source_id": "2156e051-53e8-4a30-818f-11e40d65442e", "opinion_unit_id": "7f54449c-f7bd-4842-b932-9b45201458a2", "platform": "twitter", "url": "https://x.com/FireworksAI_HQ/status/2067007202934821132", "title": "Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ", "author": "FireworksAI_HQ", "sender_type_current": "official", "source_group_current": "marketing", "source_class_current": null, "source_role_predicted": "official_brand", "source_group_predicted": "owned_official", "content_type_predicted": "technical_proof", "feedback_type_predicted": ["none"], "text": "You may have seen other platforms announce day-zero support within minutes of weights dropping, but there is a key difference between inference providers and routers.

For long‑horizon coding agents, a clean 1M window + stable infra beats leaderboard screenshots.", "rewritten_text": null, "snippet": "For long-horizon coding agents, a clean 1M window and stable infrastructure are better than leaderboard screenshots.", "engagement": 2511.0, "metrics": {"likes": 15, "views": 2494, "shares": 0, "num_comments": 2}, "created_at": "2026-06-29T07:01:10.813232+00:00", "metadata": {"processing_status": "classified", "source_type": "crawl", "content_hash": "ffa40e33b2762d586be918718e871a9bba2a1e80a83a4356aee88a46554c0aaa"}}, {"source_id": "ab4c5e54-15cf-4538-8bc5-4310e121de11", "opinion_unit_id": "ab1163dc-9368-481e-9714-f187b39cbefb", "platform": "twitter", "url": "https://x.com/lqiao/status/1806353722056053035", "title": "Fireworks AI X post by @lqiao", "author": "lqiao", "sender_type_current": "employee", "source_group_current": "creators", "source_class_current": null, "source_role_predicted": "employee", "source_group_predicted": "affiliated_insider", "content_type_predicted": "technical_proof", "feedback_type_predicted": ["none"], "text": "🔥 FireAttention v2:  speed and long context, pick two! 🔥

model path:accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2-thinking

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest, most capable version of open-source thinking model. Starting with Kimi K2, it was built as a thinking agent that reasons step-by-step while dynamically invoking tools. It sets a new state-of-the-art on Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), BrowseComp, and other benchmarks by dramatically scaling multi-step reasoning depth and maintaining stable tool-use across 200–300 sequential calls. At the same time, K2 Thinking is a native INT4 quantization model with 256k context window, achieving lossless reductions in inference latency and GPU memory usage.

## Kimi K2 Thinking API Features

## Kimi K2 Thinking FAQs

What is Kimi K2 Thinking and who developed it?

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest version of Moonshot AI's open-source “thinking model,” designed for advanced reasoning tasks. It interleaves step-by-step chain-of-thought reasoning with autonomous tool use, achieving strong performance across benchmarks like HLE, AIME25, and BrowseComp.

What applications and use cases does Kimi K2 Thinking excel at?

Kimi K2 Thinking is optimized for:

- •Agentic systems and tool-augmented reasoning
- •Coding (SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, OJ-Bench)
- •Autonomous search (BrowseComp, FinSearchComp-T3)
- •Longform writing and conversational AI
- •Enterprise RAG and complex reasoning tasks like AIME25, GPQA

What is the maximum context length for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The maximum context length is 256k tokens.

What is the usable context window for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The usable context is 256K tokens. The model maintains coherence across long sequences and supports up to 200–300 sequential tool-use steps without degradation.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support quantized formats (4-bit/8-bit)?

Yes. Kimi K2 Thinking is natively trained for INT4 quantization using Quantization-Aware Training (QAT), enabling lossless performance and up to 2x faster generation.

What is the default temperature of Kimi K2 Thinking on Fireworks AI?

The recommended default temperature is 1.0.

What is the maximum output length Fireworks allows for Kimi K2 Thinking?

Fireworks allows a maximum output of 4096 tokens per completion by default.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support function-calling schemas?

Yes. It supports OpenAI-style function-calling, and developers must provide a list of tools for each request.

How many parameters does Kimi K2 Thinking have?

The model has 1 trillion total parameters, with 32 billion active parameters per forward pass using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with 384 experts, 8 selected per token.

How are tokens counted (prompt vs completion)?

Tokens are metered as input (prompt) and output (completion) separately. Fireworks charges per 1M tokens input/output.

What license governs commercial use of Kimi K2 Thinking?

Kimi K2 Thinking is released under a Modified MIT License, permitting commercial use with some additional terms.
